---
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# What performances do donors attend?

The overlap chapter showed that most donor households also attend; this one asks what they
attend. Each registration is tagged with whether the registering household has ever given a
SUCCEEDED individual gift ("donor household"). Performance attendance is **headcount**
(succeeded ticket attendees — see [Methodology](99-methodology.html)), attributed to the
registering household; canceled, moved, and rehearsal events are excluded.

```{python}
#| label: setup-donor-att
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from hh.io import read_parquet
from hh.viz import style, BLUE, pct
from hh.analytics.productions import match_production, is_excluded, count_succeeded_attendees

regs = read_parquet("processed", "registrations_enriched.parquet")
hh = read_parquet("processed", "households_summary.parquet")

m = regs.merge(hh[["id", "is_donor"]], on="id", how="left")
m["is_donor"] = m["is_donor"].fillna(False)
perf = m[(m["event_majorcat"] == "performance") & ~m["event_name"].map(is_excluded)].copy()
perf["attendees"] = perf["tickets"].map(count_succeeded_attendees)

by_cat = perf.groupby("event_minorcat").apply(
    lambda d: pd.Series(
        {
            "attendees": d["attendees"].sum(),
            "donor attendees": d.loc[d["is_donor"], "attendees"].sum(),
        }
    ),
    include_groups=False,
).astype(int)
by_cat["donor share"] = by_cat["donor attendees"] / by_cat["attendees"]
by_cat = by_cat.reindex(["theater", "music", "opera", "dance", "other"]).dropna()
overall_share = perf.loc[perf["is_donor"], "attendees"].sum() / perf["attendees"].sum()
```

Donor households account for **`{python} f"{overall_share:.0%}"` of all performance
attendance**. By genre:

```{python}
#| label: fig-donor-share-cat
#| fig-cap: "Share of performance attendance from donor households, by genre"
fig = px.bar(
    by_cat.reset_index(),
    x="event_minorcat",
    y="donor share",
    color_discrete_sequence=[BLUE],
    custom_data=["donor attendees", "attendees"],
)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False, xaxis_title="", yaxis_title="Attendance from donor households")
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".0%", range=[0, 1])
fig.update_traces(
    hovertemplate="%{y:.0%} (%{customdata[0]:,} of %{customdata[1]:,} attendees)<extra></extra>"
)
style(fig).show()
```

```{python}
#| label: tab-donor-share-cat
by_cat.style.format({"attendees": "{:,}", "donor attendees": "{:,}", "donor share": pct})
```

**Donor households are the majority of every genre's audience — highest for music
(`{python} f"{by_cat.loc['music', 'donor share']:.0%}"`), lowest for dance
(`{python} f"{by_cat.loc['dance', 'donor share']:.0%}"`), with theater at
`{python} f"{by_cat.loc['theater', 'donor share']:.0%}"`.** Selling tickets and cultivating
donors are not separate audiences at Hubbard Hall.

## Which offerings drew the most donor attendance?

Attendance from donor households by offering — production titles where the run mapping applies
(recurring titles such as Winter Carnival aggregate across their annual runs), otherwise the
event's own name:

```{python}
#| label: tab-top-donor-offerings
perf["offering"] = perf["event_name"].map(lambda n: match_production(n) or n)
g = perf.groupby("offering").agg(
    genre=("event_minorcat", "first"),
    first_year=("starts_on", lambda s: int(s.dt.year.min())),
    attendees=("attendees", "sum"),
)
g["donor attendees"] = perf[perf["is_donor"]].groupby("offering")["attendees"].sum()
g["donor attendees"] = g["donor attendees"].fillna(0).astype(int)
g["donor share"] = g["donor attendees"] / g["attendees"]
top = g.sort_values("donor attendees", ascending=False).head(15)
top.index.name = None
top.style.format(
    {"attendees": "{:,}", "donor attendees": "{:,}", "donor share": pct}
)
```

The list is led by the big ensemble musicals and the summer operas — the same shows that fill
the house generally — plus Winter Carnival of New Work, whose audience is the most
donor-heavy of any large offering.

Do donor and non-donor households split their activity differently? Registration mix (all event
types, registrations rather than headcount):

```{python}
#| label: tab-donor-mix
mix = (
    m.groupby([m["is_donor"].map({True: "Donor households", False: "Non-donor households"}),
               "event_majorcat"], observed=True)
    .size()
    .unstack("event_majorcat")
    .fillna(0)
)
mix = mix.div(mix.sum(axis=1), axis=0)
mix.index.name = None
mix.style.format("{:.0%}")
```

Non-donor households skew toward classes; donor households split more evenly between classes
and performances.
